summary: BUG FIX VERIFICATION COMPLETE: ForJenta AI IDE critical send prompt bug fix verified. The bug where `onSend={handleSend}` passed MouseEvent as overridePrompt causing '.trim() is not a function' error has been FIXED. All 3 instances now correctly use `onSend={() => handleSend()}`. Tested both Desktop (1920px) and Mobile (375px) viewports - typing prompt, clicking send works without page reset or error.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix verified: Lines 878, 1086, 1364 in ProjectBuilder.tsx now use `onSend={() => handleSend()}` instead of `onSend={handleSend}`
test_credentials: No auth required - mock project proj-demo-1 from localStorage
context_for_next_testing_agent: Critical bug fix verified. The send prompt flow works correctly on both desktop and mobile. Console logs show: '=== CALLING GENERATE-CODE PIPELINE ===' after clicking send, confirming the handleSend function executes properly without throwing 'trim is not a function' error. Desktop shows 'Working...' status and terminal '> Request:' message. Mobile shows 'Generating code...' and 'Processing...' status. Page does NOT reset or navigate away.

bug_verification_details.bug_description: onSend={handleSend} passed React MouseEvent as overridePrompt parameter, causing (event).trim() to throw 'is not a function' error
bug_verification_details.fix_applied: Changed all 3 instances to onSend={() => handleSend()}
bug_verification_details.verification_method: Playwright automation: typed prompt in input, clicked send, verified no console error and page stayed on builder URL

test_evidence.desktop: Console showed '=== CALLING GENERATE-CODE PIPELINE ===' confirming handleSend executed correctly
test_evidence.mobile: UI showed 'Generating code...' and 'Processing...' status confirming generation started
